commitments.text: Investing in the health and resilience of our ocean ecosystems, including by strengthening the management of our national network of Marine Parks and spending an additional $194.5 million on top of existing investments to protect the Great Barrier Reef.

measurability.reason: The commitment lacks specific numbers, target year, and baseline.

gap_findings.description: Australia's NDC lacks a specific commitment on adaptation finance.
gap_findings.ipcc_evidence: IPCC AR6 WG2 Chapter 17: adaptation finance gaps persist; developing countries need significantly more than current flows.

gap_findings.description: Australia's NDC commitment on ocean ecosystems lacks quantification and baseline.
gap_findings.ndc_evidence: Investing in the health and resilience of our ocean ecosystems, including by strengthening the management of our national network of Marine Parks and spending an additional $194.5 million on top of existing investments to protect the Great Barrier Reef.
gap_findings.ipcc_evidence: IPCC AR6 WG2 Chapter 17: adaptation finance gaps persist; developing countries need significantly more than current flows.
gap_findings.quantified_gap: NDC: $194.5 million, IPCC min: $15-411 billion, gap: -99.9%

gap_findings.description: Australia's NDC commitment on ocean ecosystems is inconsistent with IPCC recommendations on adaptation finance.

details.summary: Australia's NDC lacks a specific commitment on adaptation finance, and its commitment on ocean ecosystems lacks quantification and baseline, contradicting IPCC recommendations.

pass_c.country_summary: Australia's NDC lacks a specific commitment on adaptation finance, and its commitment on ocean ecosystems lacks quantification and baseline, contradicting IPCC recommendations.
pass_c.policy_recommendation: Australia should commit to a specific adaptation finance target and provide more detailed information on its adaptation finance plans, including quantification and baseline.
